prerequisites: A running Oracle Database instance reachable from the sync environment, with its host, port, and SID known.
A database user (username) with privileges to create and write to the target schema, created with CREATE USER and granted appropriate roles. Oracle stores the password for this account.
Network connectivity to the database host and port. If the database is not directly reachable, an SSH bastion host for the optional SSH tunnel.
Optional: a TLS/SSL certificate if the connection uses encrypted transport, and any extra JDBC URL parameters required by your environment.

authMethods.name: Database username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to Oracle with a database account (username) and its password. Oracle verifies the credentials against the account created with CREATE USER and managed via GRANT/ALTER USER. The password is stored as a secret field.

authMethods.name: TLS/SSL encryption (optional)
authMethods.summary: Optionally secure the connection with TLS/SSL by supplying an SSL certificate in the encryption settings. This protects credentials and data in transit but does not replace username/password authentication.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel (optional)
authMethods.summary: Optionally connect through an SSH bastion using either an SSH private key or an SSH tunnel user password. This only tunnels the network connection; the Oracle username and password are still required to authenticate to the database.

setupSteps.title: Create a database user and grant privileges
setupSteps.body: In Oracle, create a dedicated account with CREATE USER and grant it the privileges needed to create and write to the destination schema (for example connect and table/schema management roles). Note the username and password; these map to the username and password config fields.

setupSteps.title: Identify the database connection details
setupSteps.body: Collect the host (hostname or IP of the Oracle server), the port (Oracle listener port, commonly 1521), and the sid that identifies the target database instance. These three fields are required to build the JDBC connection.

setupSteps.title: Configure the destination connection
setupSteps.body: Enter the host, port, sid, username, and password. Optionally set schema for the default output schema and raw_data_schema for raw/staging tables. Use jdbc_url_params to append any extra connection parameters your environment requires.

setupSteps.title: Configure encryption (optional)
setupSteps.body: Under encryption, choose the desired TLS/SSL mode. If certificate-based encryption is used, provide the ssl_certificate. Leave unset for an unencrypted connection.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel (optional)
setupSteps.body: If the database is only reachable through a bastion, set tunnel_method and provide either the SSH private key (ssh_key) or the tunnel user password (tunnel_user_password). The Oracle username and password are still required for database authentication.

sources.title: Configuring Authentication - Oracle Database Security Guide
CREATE USER - Oracle Database SQL Language Reference
OracleDriver (Oracle Database JDBC Java API Reference)
